page: Amazon Web Services m5.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor. It features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities and memory performance. The server achieves top-tier results in multi-core stress-ng tests and Passmark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 4544.17 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 218.48 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Database operations and Redis SET operations perform in the top 25 percent, with Redis handling 76.75 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is average, as shown by stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores.

A notable limitation is the server's poor performance in LLM text generation, where it records only 38.07 tokens per second for a small 135M model and 5.72 tokens per second for a medium 7B model, placing it in the bottom 10 percent. This makes it unsuitable for real-time generative AI workloads without external accelerators. Conversely, its high core density, strong multi-threaded execution, and robust memory bandwidth make it highly cost-efficient for resource-dense workloads. It is well-suited for large-scale database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m5.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server featuring an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and 384.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Operating at 3.1 GHz, this server delivers strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by its high stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. While memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the top 25 percent, single-core benchmarks remain average. Furthermore, LLM text generation speeds are poor, making the server ill-suited for generative AI tasks. However, its high resource density and strong multi-threaded capabilities make it highly efficient for database hosting, software compilation, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5.metal is a bare-metal server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 384 GB DDR4 RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal server delivering high multi-core compute density and robust memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise and database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8259CL at 3.1 GHz
384.0 GB of DDR4 memory with strong read and write bandwidth performance
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU accelerators
Excellent multi-core performance for software compilation and database operations
Poor text generation speeds for large language model inference workloads
